response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically broad individual contributor and staff engineer with roughly 15+ years of software development experience, but falls significantly short of the CTO profile this role demands. He has no demonstrated executive leadership, no track record of scaling engineering organizations, and his AI/agentic experience is confined entirely to his current role started in April 2025 — far too shallow and recent for a role requiring production multi-agent systems at financial stakes. His fintech exposure (RIDE Capital) was as a developer, not a leader, and there is no evidence of governing hybrid engineering teams, setting technical roadmaps for boards, or operating regulated AI systems.
strengths: Genuine breadth across distributed systems, microservices, and event-driven architectures with real production exposure at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/second) and GISCollective
Early exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling in current role at re:cinq, indicating awareness of the agentic paradigm
Strong engineering-craft fundamentals (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) and cross-domain versatility across web, mobile, embedded, and backend systems
concerns: Zero evidence of engineering leadership at scale — no team management, no org-building, no technical leadership layer development; all roles are individual contributor or solo founder
AI and agentic systems experience is limited entirely to April 2025-present with no demonstrated production multi-agent deployment history or evaluation/observability infrastructure
No fintech infrastructure depth (credit systems, payment flows, real-time risk, compliance across jurisdictions) beyond a single developer role at a small investment tooling company

key_factors.factor: No executive or team leadership experience
AI/agentic experience too shallow and recent
